Harrowing footage has revealed the extent of the damage and chaos inflicted on Venezuela by a pair of massive earthquakes last night.   The back-to-back 7.1 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes struck near the coast, sending terrified residents racing out from swaying apartment blocks as walls collapsed and clouds of dust rose over the capital. A close friend of mine does this and the level of manipulation that goes on at these events in particular, is staggering. My friend has quoted for me several times for my travel back and forth to Asia, she has never managed to get a better deal than I can get direct from hotels or airlines, but I’ve booked hotels through her out of pity really. She talks about “the business” like she’s an international business woman when in reality she is exactly the same as the lady in this article.   • Schools cannot win. I work in a primary school & we get criticised whatever we do. We’ve had parents complaining that we let their kids out in extreme heat and we’ve also had other parents complaining that we DON’T let kids out in the heat… We’ve been accused of not letting kids drink enough but you cannot force a child to drink if they don’t want to, because that would cause complaints as well. Parents moan about having to do the school run in the snow whilst others complain when you close due to heavy snow. We’ve had parents moaning about sending their kids home sick & others who moaned that we don’t send their kids home & it turned out they were sick. It’s honestly a joke. If you want to blame anyone for the weak, fragile, non resilience in society these days, then blame the parents who are so quick to complain about schools who are just trying to do their best.

DC's latest attempt to expand their cinematic universe with Supergirl has gone down like a damp squib with critics, as they labelled the film 'ugly' and 'flat' in a string of lacklustre reviews. After building excitement with her brief cameo in last year's Superman, Milly Alcock's take on the famous heroine earned a mixed reception, with one critic even calling the film 'a dispiriting girl power disappointment.' A golden retriever named Nova has become the first dog to live full-time on a psychiatric ward - with her owner crediting the pet for saving her life. Daisy, 21, spent five years in hospital battling severe anorexia, self-harming daily and struggling to speak to those around her. But everything changed when she met Nova - who now lives alongside patients on the ward.
